chief_complaint: A 75-year-old male patient presents with sudden onset of left-sided weakness and difficulty speaking.
history: The patient has a history of atrial fibrillation, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. He has been taking warfarin for the past 5 years. He underwent coronary artery bypass grafting 2 years ago. He has a family history of stroke and has been a smoker for 30 pack-years.
exam: Vital signs: BP 160/90 mmHg, HR 110 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination reveals left-sided weakness, facial drooping, and difficulty speaking.
labs: ECG: atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. Bloods: INR 2.5, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, cholesterol 250 mg/dL. Imaging: non-contrast CT head shows acute ischemic stroke in the left middle cerebral artery territory.
correct_diagnosis: Cardioembolic stroke due to atrial fibrillation
differentials: Cardioembolic stroke due to atrial fibrillation
correct_ranking: Cardioembolic stroke due to atrial fibrillation
